---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<style>
  .hh-hero {
    background: linear-gradient(135deg, #0a0a0f 0%, #1a1a2e 60%, #16213e 100%);
    color: #e8e8f0;
    padding: 4rem 2rem 3rem;
    margin: -1rem -1rem 3rem;
    border-radius: 8px;
    position: relative;
    overflow: hidden;
  }
  .hh-hero::before {
    content: '';
    position: absolute;
    inset: 0;
    background: repeating-linear-gradient(
      90deg,
      transparent,
      transparent 60px,
      rgba(240,192,64,0.03) 60px,
      rgba(240,192,64,0.03) 61px
    );
    pointer-events: none;
  }
  .hh-hero-label {
    font-family: 'Courier New', monospace;
    font-size: 0.75rem;
    letter-spacing: 3px;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    color: #f0c040;
    margin-bottom: 0.75rem;
  }
  .hh-hero-title {
    font-size: clamp(2rem, 5vw, 3.5rem);
    font-weight: 800;
    line-height: 1.1;
    color: #ffffff;
    margin-bottom: 1rem;
  }
  .hh-hero-title span { color: #f0c040; }
  .hh-hero-sub {
    font-size: 1.05rem;
    color: #a0a0b8;
    max-width: 600px;
    line-height: 1.6;
    margin-bottom: 2rem;
  }
  .hh-stat-row {
    display: flex;
    gap: 2rem;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
    margin-bottom: 2rem;
  }
  .hh-stat {
    text-align: left;
  }
  .hh-stat-num {
    font-family: 'Courier New', monospace;
    font-size: 1.8rem;
    font-weight: 700;
    color: #f0c040;
    line-height: 1;
  }
  .hh-stat-label {
    font-size: 0.75rem;
    color: #666680;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    letter-spacing: 1.5px;
    margin-top: 2px;
  }
  .hh-cta-row {
    display: flex;
    gap: 1rem;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
  }
  .hh-btn-primary {
    display: inline-block;
    background: #f0c040;
    color: #0a0a0f;
    font-weight: 700;
    font-size: 0.9rem;
    padding: 0.65rem 1.4rem;
    border-radius: 4px;
    text-decoration: none;
    letter-spacing: 0.5px;
    transition: background 0.15s, transform 0.1s;
  }
  .hh-btn-primary:hover {
    background: #ffd060;
    transform: translateY(-1px);
    text-decoration: none;
    color: #0a0a0f;
  }
  .hh-btn-secondary {
    display: inline-block;
    background: transparent;
    color: #e8e8f0;
    font-weight: 600;
    font-size: 0.9rem;
    padding: 0.65rem 1.4rem;
    border-radius: 4px;
    border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.2);
    text-decoration: none;
    transition: border-color 0.15s, transform 0.1s;
  }
  .hh-btn-secondary:hover {
    border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.5);
    transform: translateY(-1px);
    text-decoration: none;
    color: #e8e8f0;
  }

  /* ── DELIVERABLES GRID ── */
  .deliverables-grid {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(280px, 1fr));
    gap: 1.5rem;
    margin: 2rem 0;
  }
  .deliverable-card {
    border: 1px solid #e5e7eb;
    border-radius: 8px;
    padding: 1.5rem;
    background: #ffffff;
    transition: box-shadow 0.15s, transform 0.12s;
    position: relative;
    overflow: hidden;
  }
  .deliverable-card::before {
    content: '';
    position: absolute;
    top: 0; left: 0; right: 0;
    height: 4px;
    background: var(--card-accent, #4472C4);
  }
  .deliverable-card:hover {
    box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.1);
    transform: translateY(-2px);
  }
  .deliverable-icon {
    font-size: 1.8rem;
    margin-bottom: 0.75rem;
    display: block;
  }
  .deliverable-title {
    font-size: 1.05rem;
    font-weight: 700;
    color: #1a1a2e;
    margin-bottom: 0.4rem;
  }
  .deliverable-desc {
    font-size: 0.875rem;
    color: #6b7280;
    line-height: 1.5;
    margin-bottom: 1rem;
  }
  .deliverable-link {
    font-size: 0.875rem;
    font-weight: 600;
    color: var(--card-accent, #4472C4);
    text-decoration: none;
  }
  .deliverable-link:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

  /* ── METHODOLOGY SECTION ── */
  .method-grid {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(200px, 1fr));
    gap: 1rem;
    margin: 1.5rem 0;
  }
  .method-block {
    background: #f9fafb;
    border-radius: 6px;
    padding: 1.25rem;
    border-left: 3px solid var(--mb-color, #4472C4);
  }
  .method-block-title {
    font-size: 0.8rem;
    font-weight: 700;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    letter-spacing: 1px;
    color: var(--mb-color, #4472C4);
    margin-bottom: 0.4rem;
  }
  .method-block-text {
    font-size: 0.85rem;
    color: #374151;
    line-height: 1.5;
  }

  /* ── FORMULA BOX ── */
  .formula-box {
    background: #0a0a0f;
    color: #e8e8f0;
    border-radius: 8px;
    padding: 1.5rem 2rem;
    font-family: 'Courier New', monospace;
    font-size: 0.9rem;
    line-height: 2;
    margin: 1.5rem 0;
  }
  .formula-box .accent { color: #f0c040; }
  .formula-box .muted  { color: #666680; font-size: 0.8rem; }

  /* ── TEACHING CALLOUT ── */
  .teaching-callout {
    background: linear-gradient(135deg, #fefce8, #fef9c3);
    border: 1px solid #fde68a;
    border-left: 4px solid #f0c040;
    border-radius: 6px;
    padding: 1.25rem 1.5rem;
    margin: 1.5rem 0;
  }
  .teaching-callout-title {
    font-size: 0.8rem;
    font-weight: 700;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    letter-spacing: 1px;
    color: #92400e;
    margin-bottom: 0.4rem;
  }
  .teaching-callout-text {
    font-size: 0.9rem;
    color: #78350f;
    line-height: 1.6;
  }

  /* ── DIMENSION TABLE ── */
  .dim-table {
    width: 100%;
    border-collapse: collapse;
    font-size: 0.875rem;
    margin: 1rem 0;
  }
  .dim-table th {
    background: #1a1a2e;
    color: #e8e8f0;
    padding: 0.6rem 1rem;
    text-align: left;
    font-weight: 600;
    font-size: 0.8rem;
    letter-spacing: 0.5px;
  }
  .dim-table td {
    padding: 0.6rem 1rem;
    border-bottom: 1px solid #f3f4f6;
    color: #374151;
    vertical-align: top;
  }
  .dim-table tr:hover td { background: #f9fafb; }
  .dim-dot {
    display: inline-block;
    width: 10px; height: 10px;
    border-radius: 50%;
    margin-right: 6px;
    vertical-align: middle;
  }

  .section-label {
    font-family: 'Courier New', monospace;
    font-size: 0.75rem;
    letter-spacing: 2px;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    color: #9ca3af;
    margin-bottom: 0.25rem;
  }
</style>

<!-- ── HERO ── -->
<div class="hh-hero">
  <p class="hh-hero-label">Projects · Data Storytelling</p>
  <h1 class="hh-hero-title">The Hip-Hop<br><span>Database</span></h1>
  <p class="hh-hero-sub">
    138 artists. 40 variables. A from-scratch dataset built to make
    measurement theory concrete — and to start arguments in the best possible way.
  </p>
  <div class="hh-stat-row">
    <div class="hh-stat">
      <div class="hh-stat-num">138</div>
      <div class="hh-stat-label">Artists</div>
    </div>
    <div class="hh-stat">
      <div class="hh-stat-num">40</div>
      <div class="hh-stat-label">Variables</div>
    </div>
    <div class="hh-stat">
      <div class="hh-stat-num">6</div>
      <div class="hh-stat-label">Eras</div>
    </div>
    <div class="hh-stat">
      <div class="hh-stat-num">8</div>
      <div class="hh-stat-label">Lyrical Styles</div>
    </div>
    <div class="hh-stat">
      <div class="hh-stat-num">19</div>
      <div class="hh-stat-label">Deceased</div>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="hh-cta-row">
    <a href="../../hiphop/hiphop_periodic_table.html" class="hh-btn-primary">Open Periodic Table →</a>
    <a href="../../hiphop/hiphop_dashboard.html" class="hh-btn-secondary">Open Dashboard →</a>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- ── DELIVERABLES ── -->
<p class="section-label">Explore</p>
## What's in this project

<div class="deliverables-grid">

<div class="deliverable-card" style="--card-accent:#f0c040;">
  <span class="deliverable-icon">⬡</span>
  <div class="deliverable-title">The Periodic Table</div>
  <div class="deliverable-desc">
    138 artists arranged as chemical elements — grouped by lyrical style,
    ordered by era, assigned a unique atomic weight. Click any element
    for the full artist profile. Filter by region, era, or confidence tier.
  </div>
  <a href="../../hiphop/hiphop_periodic_table.html" class="deliverable-link">Open →</a>
</div>

<div class="deliverable-card" style="--card-accent:#4472C4;">
  <span class="deliverable-icon">📊</span>
  <div class="deliverable-title">The Analytical Dashboard</div>
  <div class="deliverable-desc">
    Six interactive tabs walking through construct validity, sampling bias,
    the aggregation problem, uncertainty communication, and visualization
    design — all using the same underlying dataset.
  </div>
  <a href="../../hiphop/hiphop_dashboard.html" class="deliverable-link">Open →</a>
</div>

<div class="deliverable-card" style="--card-accent:#70AD47;">
  <span class="deliverable-icon">📁</span>
  <div class="deliverable-title">The Excel Database</div>
  <div class="deliverable-desc">
    The living source file: 40 columns including five lyricism dimension scores,
    Billboard chart data, Grammy and award counts, career span, platinum albums,
    atomic weight formula, deceased status, and confidence flags.
  </div>
  <a href="../../hiphop/data/hiphop_artists.xlsx" class="deliverable-link" download>Download .xlsx →</a>
</div>

</div>

---

<!-- ── METHODOLOGY ── -->
<p class="section-label">Methodology</p>
## How the data was built

This dataset was built in three layers. The first layer is subjective: five lyricism
dimensions scored 1–10 using published critical analysis, academic literature, and
lyrical scholarship as source material. The second layer is objective: verifiable
commercial and biographical data drawn from Billboard, RIAA, and award body records.
The third layer is derived: composite scores, atomic weights, and confidence flags
calculated from the first two.

<div class="method-grid">
  <div class="method-block" style="--mb-color:#4472C4;">
    <div class="method-block-title">Rhyme Density</div>
    <div class="method-block-text">Internal rhyme complexity, multisyllabic patterns, and technical flow construction.</div>
  </div>
  <div class="method-block" style="--mb-color:#70AD47;">
    <div class="method-block-title">Vocab Breadth</div>
    <div class="method-block-text">Lexical diversity, unique word usage, and linguistic range across recorded output.</div>
  </div>
  <div class="method-block" style="--mb-color:#ED7D31;">
    <div class="method-block-title">Storytelling</div>
    <div class="method-block-text">Narrative coherence, character construction, and scene-building across tracks.</div>
  </div>
  <div class="method-block" style="--mb-color:#9B59B6;">
    <div class="method-block-title">Metaphor / Imagery</div>
    <div class="method-block-text">Figurative language density, originality, and conceptual reach.</div>
  </div>
  <div class="method-block" style="--mb-color:#C0392B;">
    <div class="method-block-title">Conceptual Depth</div>
    <div class="method-block-text">Thematic ambition, philosophical weight, and consistency of ideas across a body of work.</div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="teaching-callout">
  <div class="teaching-callout-title">Teaching concept — Construct Validity</div>
  <div class="teaching-callout-text">
    "Lyricism" is a single-word construct that we decomposed into five measurable dimensions.
    That choice is not neutral — it reflects assumptions about what counts as lyricism and
    whose critical tradition we used to define it. This is the construct validity question
    that underlies every KPI, survey instrument, and performance rating in your organization.
  </div>
</div>

---

<!-- ── ATOMIC WEIGHT ── -->
<p class="section-label">Formula</p>
## The atomic weight

Every artist has a unique atomic weight — a continuous value that works like an element's
atomic number, but is calculated rather than assigned. It can accommodate new artists
without renumbering everyone else.

<div class="formula-box">
  <span class="accent">Atomic Weight</span> = (Career Span × Composite Score) + Total Awards<br>
  &nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;+ (Birth Year ÷ 100,000) + (Hot 100 Entries ÷ 10,000,000)<br>
  <br>
  <span class="muted">— The last two terms are tiebreakers only, invisible at 4 decimal places.</span><br>
  <span class="muted">— Career Span = Last album year − Debut year (minimum 1).</span><br>
  <span class="muted">— Total Awards = Grammy wins + BET Hip-Hop wins + MTV VMA wins + AMA wins.</span>
</div>

The formula rewards sustained artistic output over raw peak performance. Rakim (#1 at
281.62) has no Grammys and modest chart presence — he ranks first because a 32-year
career multiplied by an 8.8 composite accumulates more weight than a short brilliant
run. Biggie, with a 9.0 composite and only a 4-year career, sits at 38.02. That tension
is the point.

---

<!-- ── CONFIDENCE ── -->
<p class="section-label">Transparency</p>
## Documented uncertainty

Every artist carries a confidence flag — High, Medium, or Low — reflecting the quality
of critical documentation available when the lyricism scores were assigned.

<table class="dim-table">
  <thead>
    <tr><th>Flag</th><th>Meaning</th><th>Example artists</th></tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td><span style="font-family:monospace;background:#1a3d1a;color:#4ecb71;padding:2px 8px;border-radius:3px;font-size:0.8rem;">H</span></td>
      <td>Substantial academic and critical documentation. Scores are well-grounded.</td>
      <td>Kendrick Lamar, Rakim, Nas, Eminem, OutKast</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><span style="font-family:monospace;background:#3d2e00;color:#f0c040;padding:2px 8px;border-radius:3px;font-size:0.8rem;">M</span></td>
      <td>Moderate documentation. Scores are reasonable but provisional.</td>
      <td>Cam'ron, J.I.D, Doechii, Smino, Maxo Kream</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td><span style="font-family:monospace;background:#3d1a00;color:#ff8040;padding:2px 8px;border-radius:3px;font-size:0.8rem;">L</span></td>
      <td>Thin critical record. Scores are estimates only — treat with caution.</td>
      <td>GloRilla, Mavi, Zach Fox, Yaya Bey, Dana Dane</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

<div class="teaching-callout">
  <div class="teaching-callout-title">Teaching concept — Communicating Uncertainty</div>
  <div class="teaching-callout-text">
    Most dashboards present numbers without confidence levels. This one doesn't.
    Where in your organization's reporting does uncertainty get hidden?
    What decisions are being made on L-confidence data presented as H-confidence?
  </div>
</div>

---

::: {style="text-align: center; padding: 3rem 0 1rem; color: #6b7280; font-size: 0.875rem;"}
Built with R · Quarto · readxl · ggplot2 · plotly · reactable
:::
